name: VIRCO MFG CORPORATION
business: Virco designs, produces, and distributes high-value furniture for the education market, primarily K-12 schools, and is the largest domestic manufacturer of movable educational furniture and equipment in the United States. It also serves convention centers, hospitality, government, and places of worship, and sells to wholesalers, distributors, and retailers.

exposures.sentence: Virco has continued to invest in automation at its domestic manufacturing facilities.
Reliance on third-party carriers can expose the Company to freight rate volatility, fuel surcharges, and to capacity constraints in the transportation industry.

Beginning in 2025, the United States implemented and proposed significant changes to trade policies, including broad-based tariffs on imports from certain countries and product categories, including higher tariffs on steel, aluminum, and certain manufactured goods, including furniture.

catalysts.event: Lease extension for Torrance facility until September 30, 2030
catalysts.sentence: In the second quarter of fiscal 2025, the Company executed a five-year extension of this lease expiring on September 30, 2030.

catalysts.event: Maturity of Revolving Credit and Security Agreement with PNC Bank
catalysts.sentence: The scheduled maturity date of the Restated Credit Agreement is April 15, 2027, at which point the principal amount outstanding under the Restated Credit Agreement and any accrued and unpaid interest is due and payable.

catalysts.event: Expected settlement of VIP Plan termination through lump sum distributions in Q4 fiscal 2027
catalysts.sentence: The termination is expected to be settled through lump sum distributions to participants funded by the liquidation of assets held in a rabbi trust, which are expected to occur during the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2027.
